title: Every view needs a visual focal point
summary: AI-generated layouts distribute visual weight evenly across sections without a clear entry point, leaving users unsure where to look first.
instruction: Design each view with one dominant visual focal point. Secondary elements must support rather than compete with the focal element.
rationale: A page where everything is equally prominent communicates that nothing is important. The eye needs a visual anchor to establish reading order. Without a focal point, users scan aimlessly or abandon the page.

violations: A marketing section with heading, illustration, three feature cards, testimonial, and CTA all at equal visual weight.
A dashboard with four KPI cards of identical size, color, and density - user must read all labels to find the relevant metric.
controls: A landing page with a large hero image as focal point, headline overlaid, single CTA. Feature cards below are visually lighter.
A dashboard highlighting the most important metric card with slightly larger size, accent border, and elevation.
borderline: A product listing grid with equal card sizes where the focal point is the page title rather than a single card.

exceptions.description: Search results, product grids, and index pages where equal weight across items is intentional. Admin panels used by experienced operators who scan for known data.

detector.signalDescription: Segment page into bounding boxes for major content regions. Compare area, contrast ratio, border weight, and elevation between top elements.
limitations: Cannot distinguish intentional flat layouts (gallery pages) from unintentional flatness.
A focal point may be established through typography rather than container size.

scenarios: A grid-based portfolio where equal image sizes let the work compete.
A settings page where all sections have equal weight intentionally.
mitigations: Require at least one element with area 1.5x the median or 3:1+ contrast versus other sections. Allow suppression for index and gallery layouts.

fixturePlan.description: A features page with four equal-sized cards in a 2x2 grid, identical styling, no emphasis.
A product page with a large hero image (60% viewport width) as focal point, smaller cards below.
An e-commerce grid with equal cards but a strongly styled page title providing the focal point.

notes: Normalization note: status changed from review-ready to researched. Visual feasibility is correct. The 1.5x median area heuristic is arbitrary and may not hold across different layout types. Actual focal point detection requires rendered visual analysis (contrast, size, position, whitespace). This rule is better suited for the planned visual review pipeline than static linting.
